howItWorks.intro: This motor horsepower calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How is horsepower calculated from torque?
faq.answer: HP = (Torque in ft-lbs x RPM) / 5252. The constant 5252 comes from converting ft-lbs/min to horsepower (33,000 ft-lbs/min = 1 HP, and 33000 / (2 x pi) = 5252).

faq.question: What is typical motor efficiency?
faq.answer: Standard motors are 85-90% efficient. Premium efficiency motors reach 92-96%. Efficiency drops at partial loads — a motor running at 50% load may only be 80% efficient.
